resources.string: Loading your local experience and choosing the right starting point.
A calm, local-first space for psychological self-reflection through the ten sephirot.
Onboarding, navigation, bilingual structure, and placeholders for the flows we will build next.
Review saved runs and compare future retakes once persistence is wired in.
Read the deeper context behind the Tree of Life in psychologically grounded language.
Prepare for English and Spanish support, privacy framing, and future local preferences.

Numyah Mind helps you reflect on your current experience through the Tree of Life. It turns each sephira into grounded human themes such as boundaries, vitality, emotional flow, discernment, compassion, and daily action.\n\nYou are not here to be labeled. You are here to notice patterns, understand yourself with more nuance, and continue with greater clarity.

You will move through the ten sephirot one at a time: Malkhut, Yesod, Hod, Netzach, Tiferet, Gevurah, Chesed, Binah, Chokhmah, and Keter.\n\nBefore each section, you will get a short introduction in practical language. Then you will answer a small set of questions with a simple rating scale designed to feel calm and manageable on mobile.

Your results are described through three tendencies: balance, deficiency, and excess.\n\nBalance points to a more integrated expression. Deficiency suggests an area may be under-supported or harder to access right now. Excess suggests an area may be overextended or carrying more force than is helpful. These are current tendencies, not fixed identity statements.

When you finish, you will receive a full Tree of Life profile across all ten sephirot.\n\nYou will be able to open each area for a fuller interpretation, including healthy expression, deficiency patterns, excess patterns, and suggested practices. When a result is mixed, the language should stay soft and reflective instead of forcing a hard label.
Private, local, and ready for deeper learning

Your core experience is local to your device. Progress is saved privately, and the main assessment flow is designed to work offline.\n\nThis introduction keeps things simple. If you want deeper context later, you will be able to explore that in Learn and in each sephira result.
A calm introduction before you begin.
The Tree of Life, translated into lived experience
The questionnaire and sephira intros come next. This route already exists so we can connect the app shell before scoring and question content are finalized.
Illustration for the current sephira assessment intro
A small note before you begin
This reflection works best with honesty

This reflection is most useful when your answers are honest about how things feel today.\n\nThere are no right answers here. You do not need to present your best self, only your real current experience.
What you will reflect on here
Your relationship with material life looks fairly grounded right now.
Your answers suggest Malkuth may be under-supported right now.
Your answers suggest Malkuth may be carrying too much force right now.
Your answers lean toward a grounded relationship with material life.
Your answers lean toward under-support in material life.
Your answers lean toward overextension in material life.
In practical terms, this points to a more stable connection with the body, possessions, and daily reality. You may be able to care for material life without becoming ruled by it.

In practical terms, this can mean the body, resources, or everyday structure are receiving less attention than they need. It does not mean failure. It suggests this area may need more care, steadiness, and presence.

In practical terms, this can mean money, possessions, control, or physical routines are taking up more space than is helpful. It suggests the material world may be carrying too much emotional weight right now.
This result is relatively balanced, but not strongly fixed. It suggests a generally grounded pattern with room for continued observation.
This result is not absolute. It suggests a current tendency to feel less rooted in the body, practical life, or material support.
This result is not absolute. It suggests a current tendency for material concerns or bodily control to take up more space than they need to.
Your answers formed a clear pattern in this section.
Your answers formed a noticeable pattern, with some mixed signals.
Your answers were more mixed here, so this result is best read as a gentle direction rather than a hard label.
This may show up as steady care for health, realistic management of money or possessions, and a sense that practical life supports you rather than pulls you away from yourself.
This may show up as neglecting the body, avoiding practical responsibilities, feeling disconnected from material stability, or treating basic needs as less important than everything else.
This may show up as overfocusing on control, productivity, possessions, image, or bodily discipline, to the point that material life starts to crowd out ease, flexibility, or emotional space.
Keep noticing what helps you stay grounded without becoming rigid. Small, repeatable habits are more useful here than pushing for perfection.
A gentle next step is to strengthen one basic act of care: food, rest, movement, money awareness, or tending to your environment. The goal is not intensity. It is steadiness.
A gentle next step is to loosen one area of overcontrol and ask what would feel supportive instead of demanding. The goal is not to abandon structure, but to make it more humane.
The assessment could not be loaded right now.
Your answer could not be saved. Please try again.
The assessment could not continue right now. Please try again.
The previous question could not be restored right now. Please try again.
Your progress is saved as you go. If you return home now, you can continue this reflection later.
This screen will evolve into the full Tree of Life profile with ten sephirot, softened confidence language, and deeper interpretation.
Finish the current reflection to see your saved Tree of Life overview here.
Results are not available right now
Your saved results could not be loaded right now. Please try again.
%1$d of %2$d sephirot have saved results in this completed assessment.
Completed assessments and retakes will appear here once local persistence for sessions and results is added.
This area will hold the richer educational content adapted from the source document, separate from the short onboarding flow.
The shell is ready for language-aware UI, privacy guidance, and future local preferences. For now, app language follows the device locale automatically.
English and Spanish strings are both available. More settings can be added later without changing the navigation structure.
Ready for the next feature slice
